children.title: Introduction
children.content:
# Introduction to Angular

Angular is a platform for building mobile and desktop web applications. It's built with TypeScript and provides a complete solution for building scalable applications.

## What is Angular?

Angular is a TypeScript-based open-source web application framework led by the Angular Team at Google. It provides a complete toolkit for building dynamic single-page applications.

## Key Features

- **Component-Based**: Build encapsulated components that manage their own state
- **Reactive Programming**: Use RxJS for reactive data flows
- **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript support out of the box
- **Dependency Injection**: Built-in DI container for managing dependencies
- **Testing**: First-class testing support with Jasmine and Karma
- **CLI**: Powerful command-line interface for scaffolding and building

codeExamples.title: Basic Component

children.title: Routing & Navigation
children.content:
# Angular Routing & Navigation

The Angular Router enables navigation from one view to the next as users perform application tasks.

## Router Module

The Router is an optional service that presents a particular component view for a given URL. It allows you to create single-page applications with navigation without the page being reloaded as users navigate.

## Routes Configuration

Define routes using the `Routes` interface and configure them in your application. You can create nested routes to maintain a fixed layout (like a sidebar) while swapping content.

codeExamples.title: Basic Routing Setup
Nested Child Routes

children.title: Components
children.content:
# Angular Components

Components are the basic building blocks of Angular applications. A component controls a patch of screen called a view.

## Component Anatomy

Every component consists of a TypeScript class, an HTML template, and optional CSS styles. Components use the @Component decorator to define metadata.

## Standalone Components

With Angular 14+, you can create standalone components without needing to declare them in NgModules.

codeExamples.title: Component Example

children.title: Getting Started
children.content:
# TypeScript Getting Started

TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.

## Why TypeScript?

- **Type Safety**: Catch errors at compile time instead of runtime
- **Better IDE Support**: Get intelligent code completion and refactoring
- **Object-Oriented Features**: Classes, interfaces, and modules
- **Enhanced Readability**: Self-documenting code with type annotations
- **Early Error Detection**: Errors are caught during development

## Installation

Install TypeScript globally using npm: `npm install -g typescript`

codeExamples.title: TypeScript Types

children.title: Interfaces
children.content:
# TypeScript Interfaces

Interfaces define contracts within your code and allow you to define custom types. They are used to ensure type safety in TypeScript.

## Creating Interfaces

Use the `interface` keyword to define a new interface. Interfaces are purely structural and are removed during compilation.

codeExamples.title: Interface Example

children.title: Generics
children.content:
# TypeScript Generics

Generics provide a way to create reusable components that work over a variety of types rather than a single one.

## Generic Functions

Generic functions allow you to write a function that works with multiple types while still maintaining type safety.

codeExamples.title: Generic Function Example

children.title: Introduction
children.content:
# RxJS - Reactive Extensions for JavaScript

RxJS is a library for reactive programming using Observables to make it easier to compose asynchronous or callback-based code.

## Core Concepts

- **Observables**: Represent a collection of future values or events over time
- **Observers**: Functions that listen to Observable values and react to them
- **Operators**: Functions that transform Observable values or combine multiple Observables
- **Subscription**: The act of subscribing to an Observable to receive its values

## Why RxJS?

RxJS provides a powerful way to handle:
- Asynchronous operations
- Event handling
- Data streams
- Complex async flows with minimal code

codeExamples.title: Observable Example

children.title: Common Operators
children.content:
# RxJS Operators

Operators are functions that operate on Observables and return new Observables. They enable powerful data transformation and composition.

## Categories

- **Transformation**: map, flatMap, switchMap, mergeMap
- **Filtering**: filter, debounceTime, throttleTime, distinct, take
- **Combination**: merge, concat, combineLatest, withLatestFrom
- **Utility**: tap, delay, timeout

## Pipe Method

The `pipe()` method allows you to chain multiple operators together.

codeExamples.title: Map Operator
DebounceTime Operator (for Search)

children.title: Subjects
children.content:
# RxJS Subjects

A Subject is a special type of Observable that allows values to be multicasted to multiple Observers.

## Subject Types

- **Subject**: Basic Subject that multicasts to all observers
- **BehaviorSubject**: Emits the last emitted value to new subscribers
- **ReplaySubject**: Replays a specified number of values to new subscribers
- **AsyncSubject**: Emits only the last value when the Observable completes

codeExamples.title: BehaviorSubject Example

children.title: CSS Fundamentals
children.content:
# CSS Fundamentals

CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) is used to style and layout web pages — for example, to alter the font, color, size, and spacing of your content.

## Selectors

- **Element**: `p`, `div`, `h1`
- **Class**: `.classname`
- **ID**: `#idname`
- **Attribute**: `[type="text"]`
- **Pseudo-classes**: `:hover`, `:focus`, `:active`

codeExamples.title: Flexbox Layout
